name: Sweet & Sour Pork
recipeIngredient: 4 small fresh hot chilies, cut into thin rings
1 red bell pepper, cored, seeded and cut into thin strips
6 green onions, trimmed and cut into 2-inch lengths
2 tablespoons rice vinegar or distilled vinegar
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut the pork into 1/4 by 1/2 by 2-inch strips.
2. Mix the remaining ingredients together and massage into the pork.
3. Heat the peanut oil in a large wok and sauté the pork until browned on all sides. Remove from the wok and set aside.
4. Add the garlic and ginger and stir fry until heated through but not browned.
5. Add the tomato paste, soy sauce, and broth and stir together until well blended.
6. Next, add the fresh chilies, bell pepper, and onion, and stir fry until the vegetables are just heated through (no more than 2 minutes).
7. Return the meat to the pan and toss.
8. Add the green onions and toss again.
9. Add the five-spices, sugar, vinegar, and cornstarch mixture and continue to stir-fry until the sauce is somewhat thickened and glossy. You may need to add an additional teaspoon of cornstarch to achieve the desired consistency.
10. Add salt and pepper to taste. If you want a more distinct sweet and sour flavor, add more sugar and vinegar at this time. For a hotter dish, gradually add dried red pepper flakes until the desired heat is reached.
